programme.title: The Ashes Live
programme.desc: Australia v England. Coverage of the first day of the First Test in the five-match series, which takes place at Optus Stadium in Perth, as the long-awaited contest finally gets underway. England are looking to win back the urn for the first time since 2015 and haven't won the Ashes in Australia since the famous 3-1 victory in 2010/11. This is also the first time an Ashes Test has taken place at this venue

programme.title: The Ashes Live
programme.desc: Australia v England. Coverage of the second day of the First Test in the five-match series, which takes place at Optus Stadium in Perth. England have lost their last three Ashes series in Australia without winning a single Test having lost 4-0 on the last two occasions. Their last Ashes victory down under came in the Fifth and Final Test of the 2010/11 series as the visitors won by an innings and 83 runs to claim a famous 3-1 win

programme.title: The Breakdown
programme.desc: Ally McCoist, Owen Hargreaves, Joe Cole and Joleon Lescott discuss Man City's improvement, Arsenal's title credentials, Liverpool's struggles, and more

programme.title: Live Premier League
programme.desc: Burnley v Chelsea (Kick-off 12.30pm). Coverage of the top-flight match from Turf Moor. The hosts will be looking to end their miserable run against Chelsea having not beaten the London club in 11 meetings. In that run the Clarets have only managed to pick up three points from a possible 33 against their visitors, a run that stretches back to a 3-2 victory in August 2017

programme.title: Live: Quilter Nations Series
programme.sub-title: Live Rugby: Wales v New Zealand
programme.desc: Wales v New Zealand (Kick-off 3.10pm). Coverage of the match from the fourth round of fixtures, which takes place at Principality Stadium. The hosts have a wretched record against their visitors and haven't beaten them since 1953, a run that stretches back 33 matches. The last meeting between the two sides was in November 2022, with New Zealand hammering Wales 55-23 in that fixture

programme.title: Live: Quilter Nations Series
programme.sub-title: Live Rugby: Ireland v South Africa
programme.desc: Ireland v South Africa (Kick-off 5.40pm). Coverage of the match from the fourth round of fixtures, which takes place at Aviva Stadium in Dublin. The hosts have had the better of this fixture in recent years, winning four of the last five meetings between the two sides. The last time these teams met in July 2024 saw Ireland win a thrilling match 25-24 in Durban

programme.desc: France v Australia (Kick-off 8.10pm). Coverage of the match from the fourth round of fixtures, which takes place at Stade de France

programme.title: Live Test Cricket
programme.sub-title: Live Test: India v South Africa
programme.desc: India v South Africa. Coverage of the first day of the Second Test in the two-match series from ACA Stadium in Guwahati. The hosts won their last series against the West Indies with a comprehensive 2-0 victory thanks to a win by an innings and 140 runs, and also a seven-wicket success. South Africa fought back to draw a two-match series 1-1 against Pakistan in their last outing, winning the Second Test by eight wickets after losing the first by 93 runs

programme.title: Live: Quilter Nations Series
programme.sub-title: Live Rugby: Wales v New Zealand
programme.desc: Wales v New Zealand (Kick-off 3.10pm). Coverage of the match from the fourth round of fixtures, which takes place at Principality Stadium. The hosts have a wretched record against their visitors and haven't beaten them since 1953, a run that stretches back 33 matches. The last meeting between the two sides was in November 2022, with New Zealand hammering Wales 55-23 in that fixture

programme.title: Live: Quilter Nations Series
programme.sub-title: Live Rugby: Ireland v South Africa
programme.desc: Ireland v South Africa (Kick-off 5.40pm). Coverage of the match from the fourth round of fixtures, which takes place at Aviva Stadium in Dublin. The hosts have had the better of this fixture in recent years, winning four of the last five meetings between the two sides. The last time these teams met in July 2024 saw Ireland win a thrilling match 25-24 in Durban

programme.sub-title: Live ODI New Zealand v West Indies
programme.desc: New Zealand v West Indies. Coverage of the third and final ODI in the series from Seddon Park in Hamilton. The hosts are searching for their third successive ODI series victory against the West Indies having won the previous two in 2017 and 2022. The last series three years ago saw the visitors win the first game by five wickets before New Zealand fought back to win the last two games and claim a 2-1 victory
